title: Dart Classes and Objects – Complete Beginner to Advanced Guide

meta.title: Dart Classes & Objects Tutorial (With Examples & Constructors)
meta.description: Master Dart classes and objects with real examples. Learn constructors, methods, getters, setters, static members, and OOP concepts step-by-step.

content.value: What is a Class in Dart?
A class is a blueprint used to create objects. It defines properties (variables) and behaviors (methods). In Dart, everything is treated as an object, making classes a core concept of the language.
Basic Class Example
A class is defined using the `class` keyword. It contains fields (data) and methods (functions).
Creating Objects (Instances)
Objects are created using constructors. The `new` keyword is optional in Dart.
Constructors in Dart
Constructors initialize objects. If not defined, Dart automatically provides a default constructor.
Parameterized Constructor
Named Constructors
Named constructors allow multiple ways to create objects.
Using the `this` Keyword
`this` refers to the current instance of the class and is used to avoid naming conflicts.
Getters and Setters (Important Fix)
Use private variables with getters and setters to control access and validation.
Static Members
Encapsulation in Dart
Dart uses library-level privacy. Variables starting with `_` are private to the file.
Complete Real-World Example
Best Practices

value: - Always use constructor shorthand when possible

faq.question: Can a class be private in Dart?
faq.answer: Dart doesn't have `private` keywords. Instead, library‑level privacy is achieved by prefixing an identifier with `_`. If you want a class to be private to a file, place it in its own file and don't export it.

faq.question: What is the difference between a class and an object?
faq.answer: A class is a blueprint (definition), while an object is an instance created from that blueprint. For example, `Person` is a class; `var alice = Person('Alice', 30)` creates an object `alice`.

faq.question: Can a class extend multiple classes?
faq.answer: No, Dart supports single inheritance – a class can extend only one superclass. However, you can implement multiple interfaces and use mixins to reuse code from multiple sources.

faq.question: What is the purpose of a constructor?
faq.answer: A constructor initializes a new instance of a class. It sets initial values for fields and performs any setup required before the object is used.

faq.question: How do you make a variable read‑only from outside the class?
faq.answer: Declare it as `final` (if set once) or make it private (`_var`) and provide only a getter (no setter).
